title: Barrier coated substrates
abstract: A method for forming a composite barrier layer on a substrate to protect against moisture and gas permeation, the method comprising forming onsaid substrate a composite barrier layer comprising: (i) a polymeric sublayer formed by atmospheric pressure plasma enhanced chemical vapour deposition; and (ii) an inorganic sublayer formed by atmospheric pressure spatial atomic layer deposition. Also described area substrate having such a composite barrier layer formed thereon together with an OLED opto-electronic device comprising such a substrate.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A method for forming a composite barrier layer on a substrate to protect against moisture and gas permeation, the method comprising forming on said substrate a composite barrier layer comprising : (i) a polymeric sublayer formed by atmospheric pressure plasma enhanced chemical vapour deposition; and (ii) an inorganic sublayer formed by atmospheric pressure spatial atomic layer deposition. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein the composite barrier layer formed on the substrate has a thickness of up to 20 nm. 3. A method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the polymeric sublayer has a thickness of up to 10 nm. 4. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the inorganic sublayer has a thickness of up to 1 nm. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the polymeric layer is formed on the substrate and the inorganic sublayer is formed on the polymeric layer. 6. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein multiple alternating polymeric and inorganic sublayers are formed on the substrate . 7. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the polymeric sublayer is formed by atmospheric pressure plasma deposition using pulsed plasma. 8. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the polymeric sublayer is formed by polymerisation of a monomer which is an unsaturated haloalkyl containing compound. 9. A method according to claim 5, wherein the monomer comprises an acrylate compound of formula CH 2 =CR 1 C (0)0 (CH 2 ) n R 2 where n is an integer from 1 to 10, R 1 is hydrogen or Ci_ 6 alkyl and R 2 is an alkyl or haloalkyl group . 10. A method according to claim 9, wherein the monomer comprise 1H, 1H, 2H, 2H-heptadecafluorodecyl acrylate or 1H,1H,2H,2H- perfluorododecyl acrylate. 11. A method according to any preceding claim, wherein the inorganic sublayer comprises a metal oxide, a metal nitride or metal oxynitride and combinations thereof. 12. A method according to claim 11, wherein the inorganic sublayer comprises aluminium oxide, A1 2 0 3 13. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the substrate comprises a flexible polyester polymer material . 14. A method according to claim 12 wherein the substrate comprises poly ( ethylene terephthalate) (PET) or poly ( ethylene naphthalate (PEN) . 15. A substrate having formed thereon a composite barrier layer prepared by a method according to any one of claims 1 to 1 . 16. An opto-electronic device comprising a substrate according to claim 15. 16. Use of a substrate having a composite barrier layer formed thereon according to claim 15 to prepare an opto-electronic device .
